fields.subheadline: LTL freight from New York, NY to Houston, TX covers approximately 1,673 miles with estimated transit of 5–7 business days. WARP's carrier network on this corridor includes 10+ providers with cross-dock facilities at NY/NJ Metro Hub, Philadelphia Terminal, Columbus Terminal. Get instant lane-specific quotes, compare carriers, and book in minutes.
fields.body-content:
LTL Lane Overview: New York to Houston

The New York to Houston corridor is a 1,673-mile LTL freight lane connecting New York, NY and Houston, TX. Estimated transit on this route is 5–7 business days under standard conditions.

Common equipment types on this lane include Dry Van and Reefer (temp-controlled). Palletized freight with standard dock-height pickup and delivery is the primary use case.

Nearby cross-dock and terminal facilities serving this corridor: NY/NJ Metro Hub, Philadelphia Terminal, Columbus Terminal, Houston Terminal, Dallas Mega Hub.

Congestion peaks around holidays. Winter weather Dec–Mar.

Why WARP Fits the New York to Houston LTL Lane

WARP supports ltl freight on this corridor with 10 active carriers, live tracking from pickup through delivery, and transparent per-shipment pricing. For shippers with recurring or appointment-driven freight on this route, WARP provides consistency that traditional broker models often lack.

  • Palletized freight fit — Standard pallet positions with accurate dimensional billing and weight-based pricing.
  • Appointment and scheduling — Dock-scheduled pickups and deliveries with appointment confirmation and arrival tracking.
  • Live visibility — Real-time scan events at pickup, in-transit, and delivery so you know where freight is without calling anyone.
  • Carrier network depth — 10 carriers with established routes on the New York–Houston corridor, scored on on-time performance and service quality.
  • Per-pallet economics — Transparent per-pallet and per-shipment pricing without hidden accessorial markups. Recurring lanes see rate consistency over time.

Operating Details: New York to Houston LTL

Key operational considerations for this 1,673-mile lane:

  • Transit window — 5–7 business days. Actual transit depends on pickup schedule, carrier routing, and weather conditions.
  • Shipment profile — Standard LTL dock-height pickup and delivery. Palletized freight up to 15,000 lbs. Non-standard requirements (liftgate, inside, residential) available as quoted accessorials.
  • Freight class considerations — NMFC classification directly impacts pricing. Higher-density freight typically receives more favorable rates on this lane.
  • Appointment coordination — WARP handles pickup and delivery appointment scheduling. Dock windows are confirmed and tracked with real-time arrival notifications.
  • Cross-dock routing — Freight on this lane may transfer through NY/NJ Metro Hub or Philadelphia Terminal depending on carrier routing and service level. Direct and transfer options are compared automatically.
  • Exception handling — Exception alerts within 30 minutes of status change. Exceptions escalate within minutes, not hours.
  • Service notes — Estimated 5–7 business day transit window. Carrier performance data informs routing decisions on every shipment.

LTL Pricing: New York to Houston

Estimated LTL rates on this 1,673-mile lane: $7,354 – $9,805.

Key cost factors on this corridor:

  • Distance and linehaul — At 1,673 miles, per-mile linehaul is the primary cost component. Lane density and carrier availability influence base rates.
  • Per-pallet pricing — Rates are calculated per pallet position. Consolidation with other shipments on this corridor can reduce per-unit cost.
  • Freight class impact — NMFC classification affects LTL pricing. Higher-density, lower-class freight receives more favorable rates.
  • Recurring volume — Consistent volume on the New York–Houston lane builds carrier familiarity and can stabilize rates over time.
  • Seasonal demand — Congestion peaks around holidays. Winter weather Dec–Mar.
  • Accessorials — Liftgate, inside delivery, residential pickup, limited-access, and other accessorials are quoted transparently as add-ons to the base rate.

These are modeled estimates, not guaranteed quotes.

Validate This Lane

Validate this lane with a controlled pilot: New York, NY to Houston, TX. Track quote response time, transit predictability, and exception rate across 10 active carriers on this 1673-mile corridor. Equipment includes Dry Van and Reefer (temp-controlled). Start with this single lane, measure results, and expand based on data.

fields.faq-schema:
Frequently Asked Questions

How long does LTL freight take from New York to Houston?

Estimated transit for LTL freight on this 1673-mile lane is 5–7 business days. Actual transit depends on pickup schedule, carrier routing, and weather conditions.

How much does LTL shipping from New York to Houston cost?

Estimated LTL rates on this lane range from $7,354 to $9,805 depending on weight, freight class, pallet count, and seasonal demand. These are modeled estimates — get an exact quote by entering your shipment details.

How fast can we launch a LTL pilot from New York to Houston?

Most shipping teams can scope a single-lane pilot and begin quoting within days. Start with this corridor, measure quote speed and transit reliability, then expand based on results.

How does WARP handle tracking on the New York to Houston lane?

WARP provides real-time visibility with scan events at pickup, in-transit checkpoints, and delivery confirmation. Exception alerts fire within 30 minutes of any status change so you can resolve issues before they impact customers.

What equipment is available for LTL freight from New York to Houston?

Common equipment on this lane includes Dry Van, Reefer (temp-controlled). Equipment availability varies by season and demand — WARP's carrier network provides multiple options for your freight profile.
